name: brand-icon-package
description: Exercises the one-image app-icon pipeline through native package: a single square PNG source wired in the manifest, a distributable macOS bundle whose icon artifacts are all generated from it, and no prebuilt icon containers anywhere.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the manifest is app.zon, the view src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). The counter app itself is fine as it is — this task is about shipping it.

Requirements:
- The app must ship with a real app icon. Brand policy: icon sources live at exactly assets/brand-icon.png — a single square PNG — and every platform's icon artifacts are generated from that one image. The approved artwork is the square PNG the scaffold already ships; it just lives at the wrong path for brand policy.
- No prebuilt icon containers (.icns, .ico) may appear anywhere in the project or be listed as icon sources; the single PNG is the only icon input.
- Produce a distributable macOS app bundle inside the workspace, at exactly zig-out/package/brand-icon-package.app. The bundle must carry a generated macOS icon wired into its metadata so the Dock and Finder show the brand mark.
- Verify the icon source passes the SDK's own validation (`native validate`), and keep `zig build test` green.

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes, state where the distributable bundle landed, and summarize how the icon is produced.

checks.description: the manifest sources icons from the single brand PNG
no prebuilt icon container is listed as an icon source
the packaged bundle's metadata wires an app icon
the bundle carries a generated AppIcon.icns (icns container magic present)

criteria: The manifest is the single source of truth for the icon: .icons lists exactly the one square PNG at assets/brand-icon.png, and nothing else about the manifest was broken or gratuitously changed in the process.

The icon path is honest one-image pipeline usage: the PNG source drives the generated macOS artifacts through the SDK's packaging, with no hand-built .icns, no external icon tooling wired into the project, and no committed generated artifacts pretending to be sources.
The workspace stays healthy: the app still builds and its tests still pass, and the packaging work did not leak junk files or manifest hacks into the project.
checks.description: quality of the icon-pipeline usage beyond compliance
